content_hints.description: Cassalanter Villa is an extravagant manse with dozens of lavish chambers, but its splendor belies the terrible secret it holds. The temple of Siamorphe that once lay beneath the estate has been desecrated by Victoro and Ammalia’s devil-worship, and now this great chapel is dedicated to the Lord of the Nine Hells.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the extravagant manse of Cassalanter Villa, its splendor belied by a terrible secret. The air is heavy, and you notice the lingering scent of decay mixed with the oppressive silence of a place once dedicated to a forgotten deity.

npcs.name: Urstul Floxin
npcs.role: Zhent assassin

npcs.name: Yalah Gralhund
npcs.role: Resident of Gralhund Villa

event_triggers: On entry: Sounds of fighting echo from inside -- steel on steel, a scream cut short.

content_hints.description: Urstul Floxin returned to Gralhund Villa to confront Yalah Gralhund regarding the nimblewright's meddling in his mission. Lady Gralhund, displeased with Urstul, wrested the Stone of Golorr from him at swordpoint and ordered her guards to lock him up.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the opulent halls of Gralhund Villa, where the air hangs heavy with unspoken conflict. Fractured light spills across marble floors marred by muddy bootprints, and a suit of ceremonial plate armor lies toppled near the staircase, its pieces scattered like the bones of a fallen knight.

npcs.name: Urstul Floxin
npcs.role: Zhent assassin who returned to Gralhund Villa to confront Yalah Gralhund about sending the nimblewright to meddle in his mission.

npcs.name: Yalah Gralhund
npcs.role: The person Urstul Floxin was confronting at Gralhund Villa.

npcs.name: Lady Gralhund
npcs.role: The person who wrested the Stone of Golorr from Urstul Floxin.

npcs.name: Orond
npcs.role: Lady Gralhund's husband who was providing coin and shelter to Urstul and his fellow Zhents.

event_triggers: On entry: A Zhentarim thug crashes through a door to the left, bleeding from a crossbow bolt in his shoulder.

content_hints.description: Urstul Floxin returned to Gralhund Villa to confront Yalah Gralhund regarding the nimblewright's meddling in his mission. Lady Gralhund, displeased with Urstul, wrested the Stone of Golorr from him at swordpoint and ordered her guards to lock him up.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a long oak table set for twelve with porcelain bowls and crystal goblets, but dinner was never served. Soup has gone cold in the bowls, and a crossbow bolt juts from the back of the host's chair. Glass crunches underfoot—a shattered wine decanter, its contents soaking into the carpet in a spreading stain indistinguishable from blood.

npcs.name: Urstul Floxin
npcs.role: Zhent assassin who returned to Gralhund Villa to confront Yalah Gralhund about sending the nimblewright to meddle in his mission.

npcs.name: Yalah Gralhund
npcs.role: The person Urstul Floxin was confronting at Gralhund Villa.

npcs.name: Lady Gralhund
npcs.role: The person who wrested the Stone of Golorr from Urstul Floxin.

npcs.name: Orond
npcs.role: Lady Gralhund's husband who was providing coin and shelter to Urstul and his fellow Zhents.

content_hints.description: The carnage at Gralhund Villa is significant enough that the City Watch cannot conceal what has transpired for long. Lady Gralhund’s nimblewright fled the estate with the Stone of Golorr, hiding the artifact in a secret location within Waterdeep.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the opulent interior of Gralhund Villa, where the air is thick with the earthy scent of old books. The library fills two floors of the three-story building, and the sheer volume of books seems to contain more than just knowledge.

npcs.name: Lady Gralhund
npcs.role: Lady Gralhund’s nimblewright hides the artifact in a secret location in Waterdeep.

npcs.name: Hrabbaz
npcs.role: Lady Gralhund and Hrabbaz are the only people who know where the nimblewright has gone, but they feign ignorance when questioned about it.

hazards.name: Glyph of Warding on desk drawer

event_triggers: On search (DC 14 Investigation): The journal reveals the Gralhunds have been playing the Zhentarim and Xanathar Guild against each other.

content_hints.description: Gralhund Villa is the location where Urstul Floxin returned to confront Yalah Gralhund regarding the nimblewright's meddling in his mission. Lady Gralhund, deciding she no longer liked Urstul, wrested the Stone of Golorr from him at swordpoint and ordered her guards to lock him up.
content_hints.read_aloud: You step onto a gallery landing overlooking the foyer below, the marble floor visible through the balustrade. Ornamental swords and shields hang between tall windows, one shattered outward, its curtain billowing in the night breeze.

npcs.name: Urstul Floxin
npcs.role: Zhent assassin who returned to Gralhund Villa to confront Yalah Gralhund about sending the nimblewright to meddle in his mission.

npcs.name: Yalah Gralhund
npcs.role: The person Urstul Floxin was confronting at Gralhund Villa.

npcs.name: Lady Gralhund
npcs.role: The person who wrested the Stone of Golorr from Urstul Floxin.

npcs.name: Orond
npcs.role: Lady Gralhund's husband who was providing coin and shelter to Urstul and his fellow Zhents.

content_hints.description: After fleeing Gralhund Villa with the Stone of Golorr, Lady Gralhund’s nimblewright hides the artifact in a secret location in Waterdeep, setting the stage for chapter 4. The carnage at the estate cannot be hidden from the City Watch for long, as there are too many murdered servants and guards.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see a hidden alcove opening into a space that feels wrong—the air is too still, too warm, and sounds from outside are swallowed completely. The walls are lined with dull gray sheeting that absorbs the light, and a locked strongbox beneath an altar radiates a faint, unnatural warmth against your fingertips.

npcs.name: Yalah Gralhund
npcs.role: She carries a ring of keys that open all locked doors in the mansion, as well as the locked wooden trunk at the foot of the bed.

npcs.name: Lady Gralhund
npcs.role: She wrests the Stone of Golorr from Urstul Floxin at swordpoint and orders her guards to lock him up until she decides what to do with him.

event_triggers: On discovery (DC 15 Perception): The lead-lined walls suggest the Gralhunds are hiding something from magical surveillance.

content_hints.description: Gralhund Villa is the location where the Zhent assassin Urstul Floxin returned to confront Yalah Gralhund about sending the nimblewright to meddle in his mission. Lady Gralhund decided she no longer liked Urstul and, taking advantage of his injuries, wrested the Stone of Golorr from him at swordpoint before ordering her guards to lock him up.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see narrow cots lining both walls of this long, low room, each made with military precision. Footlockers sit at the end of every bed, and uniforms hang from hooks on the wall.

npcs.name: Yalah Gralhund
npcs.role: Lady Gralhund decides she no longer likes Urstul and, taking advantage of his injuries, wrests the Stone of Golorr from him at swordpoint and orders her guards to lock him up until she decides what to do with him.

npcs.name: Orond
npcs.role: Gralhund and her husband, Orond, are providing coin and shelter to Urstul and his fellow Zhents in exchange for the promise of getting their fair share of the hidden cache of gold.

content_hints.description: Gralhund Villa is a location where Urstul Floxin returned to confront Yalah Gralhund about the nimblewright's meddling in his mission. Lady Gralhund decided she no longer liked Urstul and, taking advantage of his injuries, wrested the Stone of Golorr from him at swordpoint and ordered her guards to lock him up.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You stand before Gralhund Villa, a place steeped in shadow and intrigue. The air feels heavy with unspoken conflict, and you sense the lingering tension of a recent confrontation.

npcs.name: Urstul Floxin
npcs.role: Zhent assassin who returned to Gralhund Villa to confront Yalah Gralhund

npcs.name: Lady Gralhund
npcs.role: Owner of Gralhund Villa who wrested the Stone of Golorr from Urstul Floxin

event_triggers: On combat start: The Nimblewright fights with mechanical precision, prioritizing escape over killing. If reduced to half HP, it attempts to flee across the rooftops.
On Stone recovered: The Stone of Golorr pulses with cold light and whispers a single word: 'Vault.'
